description: Per-anon-id daily AI chat limit
Per-user daily AI chat limit
Max user message length
Max model response length
Hard token budget per user/day
Min seconds between messages from same actor
Failed logins before lockout
Lockout duration
Access-token lifetime (minutes). Range 5..1440. Changing only affects NEWLY-issued tokens; existing JWTs keep their baked-in expiry until self-expiry.
Refresh-token lifetime (days). Range 1..30. Caps the effective session length — frontend 401 auto-refresh extends a session up to this idle window before forcing re-login.
ID of the currently active llm_providers row
Fallback provider when active fails
Provider config cache TTL
ID of the LLMProviderConfig row used for RAG embeddings (must be openai-typed). Set this after configuring your OpenAI provider.
Embedding provider config cache TTL
Number of RAG chunks to retrieve per query
Minimum cosine similarity (0..1) to include a chunk in context. Below this, the chunk is silently dropped — prevents irrelevant snippets polluting the prompt.
PMI's official URL for the CPMAI course bundle. Surfaced when the user asks about registering for the actual exam. Leave blank to hide the suggestion.
PMI's Exam Content Outline URL. Surfaced when the user asks what's on the exam.

value: You are CPMAI Prep's official AI assistant. Be concise and helpful. If a user's question is outside your scope, say so directly rather than guessing.
description: Top-line persona / instruction prepended to every chat turn. Admin-tunable without redeploy.

value: CPMAI Body of Knowledge (the 6 phases), ML/AI fundamentals, data science, certification exam tactics, pricing on this site, your account on this site, our course catalog and course contents, our live class schedule and dates, any information published on this site's pages.
description: Comma-or-newline list of topics the bot will discuss freely. Folded into the system prompt as 'TOPIC SCOPE'.

value: PMP-only project-management methodologies (PRINCE2, classical waterfall PM, PMBOK process groups, agile-only frameworks unless they touch ML).
description: Topics the bot should politely decline. Folded into the system prompt as 'BANNED'.

value: PMI's history with CPMAI, the relationship between PMI and CPMAI certification, where to register for the CPMAI exam on PMI's site.
description: Additional topics the assistant may discuss freely, even if they don't appear in allowed_topics. Folded into the system prompt as 'ALSO ALLOWED'. Items here also override the banned list. Use for one-off subjects you want to unlock without expanding the main scope statement.

value: Our AI tutor is being set up right now and isn't able to answer questions yet. Please check back shortly — sorry for the wait.
description: End-user-facing message the assistant shows when no LLM provider is configured / active. Keep it generic — no mention of admin/provider/setup details.

value: Ask about courses, pricing, live classes & the CPMAI exam
description: Subtitle shown under 'CPMAI Assistant' in the chat panel header. Tells learners what the AI is grounded in.

value: Please sign in to continue chatting. Anonymous chat needs a browser identifier — refresh the page or sign in.
description: User-facing error message returned when an anonymous request hits the assistant without an anon_id cookie. Edit to friendlier copy if learners report confusion.

value: What's the difference between Phase 2 and Phase 3?
How much is the exam bundle?
When is the next live class?
Where do I register for the actual exam?
description: Starter prompts shown in the chat widget's empty state. Each entry becomes a clickable chip that pre-fills the chat input. Max 10 entries, 200 chars each. Empty list disables the suggestions.

SYSTEM prompt for the FAQ handler (factual questions about CPMAI cert process + admin-uploaded reference materials). Leave blank to use the hardcoded default (recommended starting point). Edit to tighten/widen scope, change tone, or override what the handler will/won't answer.
SYSTEM prompt for the Content handler (CPMAI concept explanations + uploaded reference materials). Leave blank to use the hardcoded default. Edit for style/tone/grounding-strictness changes.
SYSTEM prompt for the Account handler (subscription / pricing questions). Leave blank to use the hardcoded default. Edit if you want a different sales-tone or stricter privacy framing.
SYSTEM prompt for the Insights handler (personalised analysis of a learner's exam attempts). Leave blank to use the hardcoded default. Edit to shift the tone (more directive vs more encouraging).

When true, the assistant runs post-checks on every LLM response and writes drift events to audit_log (visible at /admin/assistant-drift). Detects refusals when context was available, missing citations, invented citation IDs, and empty responses. Off by default — turn on once you have ~100+ chats to look at trends.

Which intent (and therefore which handler) the classifier picks when no keyword in a question matches a known intent pattern. 'content' is the recommended default — ContentHandler has the broadest SYSTEM prompt and pulls from question_explanation + upload corpora. Other valid values: 'faq', 'account', 'insights', 'pmi_reference'.

Substrings that route a chat question to the PMI_REFERENCE handler (returns the configured PMI official URL). FIRST priority in the classifier — any of these matching short-circuits the rest of the keyword scan. Admin can edit at runtime; case is preserved in the form but compared case-insensitively. Empty list = PMI handler never matches via keyword (questions can still reach it via the agentic router or by being the admin-configured default intent).

Substrings that route to the INSIGHTS handler (queries the user's recent exam_sessions for personalised feedback). SECOND priority — runs after PMI. Watch out for generic words like 'improve' that can mis-route content questions to INSIGHTS when the user isn't asking about their own attempts.

Substrings that route to the ACCOUNT handler (RAG over the plan corpus). THIRD priority. ACCOUNT cannot read the user's actual subscription expiry / payment history — for that, flip to agentic which has an account_state tool. ACCOUNT is the right destination for plan/pricing questions only.

Substrings that route to the CONTENT handler (RAG over question_explanation + upload corpora). FOURTH priority. This is also the default catch-all when no keyword matches — see assistant.classifier.default_intent.

Substrings that route to the FAQ handler (RAG over faq + upload corpora). FIFTH priority. Add a substring here if you've added a new FAQ row and want pricing-style or process-style questions to land on this handler. For example, adding 'cost' here makes 'How Much Does CPMAI Cost?' route to the FAQ corpus where the answer lives.

Pre-text injected before the operator's allowed_exceptions list in the system prompt. Tune the language to make the LLM treat exceptions as truly in-scope (e.g. add 'do NOT decline' for models that anchor on narrower handler framing). Leave blank to use the strong default ('Do NOT decline... Do NOT apologize... if context covers it answer from context, otherwise answer concisely from general knowledge'). Only edit if you switch LLM provider or notice the bot still refusing exception topics.

Which orchestration flow handles chat turns. 'legacy' = the keyword-classifier + per-intent handler pipeline (default; unchanged behaviour). 'agentic' = LangGraph-style router + tool-calling + synthesis. 'percent:N' (e.g. 'percent:10') = N% of users get agentic by deterministic hash(anon_id|user_id+day), the rest stay on legacy — recommended for gradual rollout. 'shadow' = both flows run for the same query; legacy answer returned to user, agentic logged for comparison (~2x cost). Falls back to 'legacy' on any malformed value.

Hard cap on tool calls the router may issue per chat turn (including re-plans after empty tool results). Lower = cheaper but less recovery; higher = better at multi-tool questions but riskier on cost. 4 is a sensible default.

Admin-tunable SYSTEM prompt for the agentic router LLM (the one that picks tools). Empty = use the shipped default in code. The router prompt should describe each available tool and the persona; we ship a default that mirrors the legacy classifier's intent definitions. Tune only after observing real router behaviour in the drift dashboard.

Admin-tunable SYSTEM prompt for the agentic synthesis LLM (the one that turns tool results into the final answer). Empty = use the shipped default in code. The synthesis prompt should instruct the LLM to (a) treat tool results as evidence, not orders, (b) express uncertainty honestly when tool results are weak, (c) cite sources. Tune for refusal/hallucination patterns surfaced in the drift dashboard.

When flow='shadow', this is the fraction (0.0-1.0) of requests on which the agentic side actually runs alongside legacy. 0.0 = shadow is functionally disabled. 0.1 = 10% of legacy requests also run agentic and log the result for comparison (~10% extra LLM cost). Used to gather A/B data before flipping to a percent-rollout.
Active payment_providers row ID — INR-rail provider (typically Razorpay). Non-INR routing uses payment.non_inr_provider_id below.
Active payment_providers row ID for non-INR currencies (typically PayPal). Null = non-INR payments rejected with 503 until configured.
Payment provider config cache TTL
If true: offer code stacks on top of discount_price. If false: discount_price wins when set; offer applies to base_price only when no discount.

GST percentage applied on top of the post-offer subtotal. Computed server-side and shown as a separate line in the order summary. Set to 0 to disable. GST applies to INR-currency checkouts ONLY — international currencies skip GST automatically.

ISO-4217 codes the /pricing currency selector offers. Must include INR (Plan.base_price_paise is denominated in INR, FX is INR-relative). Add codes here AND populate the matching FX rate in pricing.fx_rates_inr_per_unit before the option becomes usable.

LEGACY admin-managed FX rates. Empty by default. The live cron writes to pricing.fx_live_raw instead — this setting is retained for backward compatibility but new deployments should leave it empty. To force a specific rate for a single currency (e.g. promo pricing), use pricing.fx_overrides.

AUTO-MANAGED: raw mid-market rates fetched from Frankfurter (ECB-published) by the daily cron. Format: {"USD": 83.33, ...} (INR per 1 unit). Admins should NOT edit this by hand — use pricing.fx_overrides instead. Empty until first cron run.
AUTO-MANAGED: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last successful FX fetch from Frankfurter. Empty until first cron run. /health surfaces an fx_stale flag when this is > 7 days old.

Markup applied on top of the live mid-market rate to cover Razorpay's ~3% international FX fee + ~2% buffer for FX drift between quote-time and capture-time. Shown transparently to international buyers as a separate 'international processing fee' line on the order summary (not buried in the rate). Range: 0-50%. Default 5% is conservative but safe; lower if you want leaner pricing AT YOUR OWN RISK.

Admin-set FX rates that WIN over the live cron (priority order: override > live > stale). Use for: (a) currencies Razorpay supports but Frankfurter doesn't cover (AED, SAR, KWD — Middle East peggers; KES, GHS, NGN — African); (b) promo pricing in a specific country at a fixed rate. Same shape as fx_live_raw: {"AED": 22.6, ...}. Markup is NOT applied to overrides — the admin's number is the final effective rate.

description: Heading shown above the lead-capture form on the landing page
Button label on the lead-capture form
Where to send the visitor after a successful lead form submission

value: Premium unlocks all advanced sets, AI tutor with extended quota, and detailed performance analytics.
description: Body of the upsell card on the learner dashboard

value: Pass the CPMAI certification on your first attempt
description: H1 headline on the public landing page (/). Keep under ~50 chars for mobile layout.

value: Realistic mock exams · AI-powered coaching · Detailed answer reasoning for every question across all 6 CPMAI phases.
description: Supporting paragraph under the H1 on the public landing page. Bullet-style sentence with middle-dots works well; mobile wraps OK up to ~140 chars.

value: Two steps to ace the exam
description: Heading of the prep-paths section under the hero. Reframe freely (e.g. 'Two ways to prepare').

value: First build deep understanding with structured courses, then prove you're exam-ready with realistic mock exams. Work through both to maximise your score.
description: Supporting sentence under the prep-paths heading. Frame as a sequence ('first… then…') to nudge visitors through both steps.
Title of the first prep-path card (links to /courses).

value: Step-by-step lessons across all 6 CPMAI phases — video, downloadable resources, and a listen-anywhere podcast.
description: Description text on the first (courses) prep-path card.
Call-to-action label on the first (courses) prep-path card.
Title of the second prep-path card (links to /exams).

value: Realistic, PMI-standard practice exams with per-question explanations and domain-level score breakdowns.
description: Description text on the second (mock exams) prep-path card.
Call-to-action label on the second (mock exams) prep-path card.

value: You're not signed in. Free sets are open — start one anonymously and you'll see your result immediately (just not saved to a dashboard). Sign in to save attempts; subscribe to unlock premium sets.
description: Banner shown on /exams when the visitor is NOT signed in. Plain text only — frontend renders the same indigo-50 styling regardless of length.
Brand name shown in the top-left of every page

value: Pass the CPMAI certification on your first attempt.
description: Short tagline shown in the footer under the brand
Public contact email shown in footer (leave blank to hide)
LinkedIn profile URL for footer (leave blank to hide)
YouTube channel URL for footer (leave blank to hide)
Twitter/X profile URL for footer (leave blank to hide)
Instagram profile URL — appears in footer + social-queue mark-posted picker. Leave blank to hide.
Facebook page URL. Leave blank to hide.
Threads profile URL. Leave blank to hide.
TikTok profile URL. Leave blank to hide.
GitHub profile/org URL. Leave blank to hide.
Privacy-contact email shown on /privacy. Falls back to site.support_email if blank.
Public contact phone (optional, displayed in footer when set).
Bottom-of-footer copyright line
Show Pricing link in the top nav (set false until plans are public)
MaxMind account ID for GeoLite2-City download (digits only — visible in MaxMind UI under My Account)
MaxMind license key for GeoLite2-City download. Generate at maxmind.com → My License Keys. Stored masked; rotated via this admin UI without redeploy.
If true, the refresh cron downloads a fresh GeoLite2-City DB when scheduled. Set false to pause refreshes (e.g. during a MaxMind outage).

Cron expression for when the refresh script should run. Default is Wednesdays and Saturdays at 04:17 UTC (aligned with MaxMind's Tue/Fri release cadence). Cron itself fires every minute on the VPS; this expression is the gate. Capped at 24 fires/day to prevent MaxMind rate-limit issues. Adjust if MaxMind changes their release schedule.
How many X-Forwarded-For hops are our own proxies, counting from the right. cpmai has 1 (Caddy). Change only when the network topology changes.

When true, the public homepage at / renders the content_page marked is_landing=true. When false (default), / always shows the marketing homepage regardless of any is_landing flag. Per-deploy switch so the operator can preview / safely toggle without rebuilding.
Zoom Meeting SDK Key (browser embed auth). Get from Zoom App Marketplace → your Meeting SDK app → Credentials. Public-ish (visible to logged-in joiners) but never useful without sdk_secret.
Zoom Meeting SDK Secret. Signs the per-session JWT — NEVER sent to the browser. Stored masked.
Zoom OAuth Server-to-Server Account ID. Used to create/update meetings via the REST API.
Zoom OAuth Server-to-Server Client ID.
Zoom OAuth Server-to-Server Client Secret. Stored masked.
Zoom account email that owns scheduled meetings (REST API uses this as the user identity for meeting creation).
Secret token for verifying Zoom webhook signatures (recording.completed etc.). Get from Zoom App Marketplace → Feature → Event Subscriptions → Secret Token. Stored masked.
How long a Web SDK signature is valid. Default 30 min — long enough for the user to click 'Join live' but short enough that a leaked signature expires fast.
Override the Zoom REST base URL. Leave blank for the default (https://api.zoom.us/v2). Only change if Zoom rolls out a regional endpoint we want to hit.
Maximum upload size in MB. Default 1024 (1 GB) — covers most hour-long lecture videos at recommended compression. Raise for raw 4K masters; lower to enforce stricter limits.

Master kill switch for the Visitor Insights SPA tracker. When false, POST /track returns a no-op and no events are written. Use to silence the firehose during incidents (DB stress, partial outage, legal hold) without a redeploy.

Fraction of batches the /track endpoint persists (0.0–1.0). 1.0 = keep everything; lower values drop batches BEFORE any DB write. Default 1.0 is fine at v1 volume; drop below 1.0 only when journey_events row growth threatens index health.

When true, /admin/insights/* dashboard endpoints prefer the pre-aggregated visitor_insights_daily rollup over live aggregation against journey_events. Flip ON only once the nightly rollup job has produced enough days of data to keep the dashboard accurate.
Reddit profile/community URL (full https:// link). Empty hides the Reddit icon in the footer + landing 'connect with me' block.
Heading above the social links shown below the landing-page lead-capture CTA. Empty hides just the label; the icons still render if any site.*_url is set.

Master switch for the lead → auto-offer reply email. When false, no transactional email is sent on landing-form sign-up. Turn ON only after SMTP creds + a default template + email.auto_offer_code are configured.

Master switch for lifecycle email automations (the mail types managed in /admin/email-automations: signup nudge, payment received, exam follow-up, …). While OFF the outbox dispatcher pauses — queued mails stay pending and send when switched back ON. Turn ON only after SMTP creds are configured and templates reviewed.
SMTP server hostname for outbound mail. Hostinger default is smtp.hostinger.com.
SMTP port. 465 = implicit SSL (set email.smtp_use_ssl=true); 587 = STARTTLS (set email.smtp_use_ssl=false).
Use implicit SSL (SMTP_SSL) on connect. True for port 465 (Hostinger); false to use STARTTLS on 587.
SMTP login username — the Hostinger mailbox address.
SMTP password for the mailbox. Stored masked (last 4 shown). Set via the Runtime Settings secret input; never round-trips in plaintext after first save.
From address on outbound emails. Should match the authenticated mailbox to pass SPF/DKIM.
Display name shown alongside the From address in the recipient's inbox.

The admin-enabled offer code advertised in the auto-reply email ({{offer_code}}). Must match an active code in /admin/offer-codes; its 24h validity (valid_until) is managed there. Empty = send the email without a code.
CTA link target in the auto-reply email ({{enroll_url}}) — e.g. the pricing/checkout page. Empty falls back to the site root.
Master switch for the live-class registration banner under the landing hero. Turn ON after setting the text + registration link below.

value: Live CPMAI exam-prep classes are open — reserve your seat!
description: Announcement text inside the live-class banner. Keep under ~120 chars so it stays one or two lines on mobile.
Registration link for live classes (calendar invite / Zoom registration / booking page). Full https:// URL. Empty shows the banner text without a button.
Label on the banner's registration button.
Banner text size in px (10–48). The button scales with it.
Banner font style: normal | italic | bold | bold-italic.
Banner text color (hex, e.g. #312e81). Pick something readable against the background color.
Banner background color (hex, e.g. #e0e7ff).
Attention animation for the banner: none | pulse (gentle fade in/out) | blink (hard on/off). Users with reduced-motion enabled never see the animation.
Show the live-class REGISTRATION button on the banner (needs live_banner_link_url set). OFF hides just this button; the banner text stays.
Registration button background (hex, e.g. #312e81). Empty = automatic (uses the banner text color, which always contrasts).
Registration button label color (hex). Empty = automatic (uses the banner background color).
Show a second ON-DEMAND TRAINING REQUEST button on the banner. Point the URL below at your Google Form for custom training details.
Label on the on-demand training button.
On-demand training request link — usually a Google Form (full https:// URL). Empty hides the button even when enabled.
On-demand button background (hex). Empty = automatic (white with a border in the banner's text color).
On-demand button label color (hex). Empty = automatic (the banner's text color).
Show/hide the testimonial carousel on the landing page. Cards are managed in /admin/testimonials; the section auto-hides when no active cards exist.
Heading above the landing-page testimonial carousel.
Auto-rotate interval for the testimonial carousel in milliseconds (2000–60000). Manual arrow clicks reset the timer.

value: Let us help you find what you were looking for:

value: Something went wrong on our end
description: Heading on the unexpected-error page.

value: Please try again — or jump back to one of these pages:
description: Supporting sentence on the unexpected-error page. Empty hides the sentence.

Show the 'here's where to go instead' block on the 404/error pages: quick links (Home, Courses, Mock exams, Pricing) plus the live-class registration button when the landing banner is enabled. OFF = just the message.
